parsed_description: This protein is predicted to function as a bifunctional enzyme involved in the de novo biosynthesis of UDP-N-acetylglucosamine (UDP-GlcNAc). It likely catalyzes the last two sequential reactions: the N-acetyltransferase activity (transferring an acetyl group from acetyl-CoA to glucosamine-1-phosphate to form N-acetylglucosamine-1-phosphate) and the diphosphorylase activity (converting N-acetylglucosamine-1-phosphate to UDP-GlcNAc using UTP). These activities are important for cell wall biosynthesis and cell morphogenesis.

parsed_description: The provided protein sequence is predicted to be a viral envelope transmembrane protein, likely functioning as a class I viral fusion protein (such as HIV-1 gp41). It contains motifs associated with membrane fusion, including heptad repeats (coiled-coil regions), and internalization signals such as YXXL and di-leucine motifs. These features facilitate viral entry by mediating fusion of the viral and host cell membranes, clathrin-dependent endocytosis, and evasion of host immune responses. The protein is associated with the viral envelope, host cell plasma membrane, and endosomal membranes, and plays a structural role in virion attachment and entry into host cells.

parsed_description: Stress-responsive chromatin regulator that plays a role in various biological processes including innate immunological memory, adipocyte differentiation, or telomerase regulation. In absence of stress, contributes to the formation of heterochromatin and heterochromatin-like structure by recruiting histone H3K9 tri- and di-methyltransferases thus silencing the transcription of target genes. Phosphorylation disrupts interactions with histone methyltransferase and enhances association with coactivators containing histone acetyltransferase and/or histone demethylase, leading to disruption of the heterochromatin-like structure and transcriptional activation. Also plays a role in maintaining epithelial regenerative capacity and protecting against cell death during intestinal epithelial damage and repair.

parsed_description: Carbohydrate-binding lectin with a preference for chitin. Has no chitinase activity. May play a role in tissue remodeling and in the capacity of cells to respond to and cope with changes in their environment. Plays a role in T-helper cell type 2 (Th2) inflammatory response and IL-13-induced inflammation, regulating allergen sensitization, inflammatory cell apoptosis, dendritic cell accumulation and M2 macrophage differentiation. Facilitates invasion of pathogenic enteric bacteria into colonic mucosa and lymphoid organs. Mediates activation of AKT1 signaling pathway and subsequent IL8 production in colonic epithelial cells. Regulates antibacterial responses in lung by contributing to macrophage bacterial killing, controlling bacterial dissemination and augmenting host tolerance. Also regulates hyperoxia-induced injury, inflammation and epithelial apoptosis in lung.

It mediates the fusion of the viral membrane with host cell membranes (including endosomal and plasma membranes), facilitating viral entry into the host cell. It also contains motifs involved in endocytosis and internalization, such as YXXL and di-leucine motifs, and plays roles in actin filament reorganization, viral protein processing, and modulation of host immune responses.

Binds to sialic acid-containing receptors on the cell surface to mediate virion attachment and entry via endocytosis. Traffics to the endoplasmic reticulum, where protein folding machinery triggers uncoating. Utilizes ER-associated degradation machinery for cytosolic translocation before nuclear entry. Encapsulates viral genomic DNA in the nucleus and participates in rearranging nucleosomes around the viral DNA. Involved in virion assembly and lytic release from host cells.

parsed_description: A helicase/nuclease that prepares double-stranded DNA breaks (DSB) for recombinational DNA repair. Binds to DSBs and unwinds DNA via a rapid and processive ATP-dependent bidirectional helicase activity. Unwinds dsDNA until it encounters a Chi sequence from the 3' direction, then cuts single-stranded DNA a few nucleotides 3' to the Chi site. Produces a long 3'-ssDNA overhang to facilitate RecA binding for homologous recombination and repair. Contributes ATPase, 3'-5' helicase, exonuclease activity, and loads RecA onto ssDNA as part of the holoenzyme.

It is involved in processes such as clathrin-dependent endocytosis, viral protein processing, and modulation of host antiviral defense. The sequence also contains motifs associated with endocytosis signaling (e.g., YXXL motif).

It displays origin-specific DNA cleavage, nucleotidyl transferase, ATPase, and helicase activities, and is involved in DNA replication and interaction with the host cell nucleus.

parsed_description: Major sphingomyelin synthase at the Golgi apparatus. Catalyzes the reversible transfer of phosphocholine moiety in sphingomyelin biosynthesis: in the forward reaction transfers phosphocholine head group of phosphatidylcholine (PC) on to ceramide (CER) to form ceramide phosphocholine (sphingomyelin, SM) and diacylglycerol (DAG) as by-product, and in the reverse reaction transfers phosphocholine from SM to DAG to form PC and CER. The direction of the reaction depends on the levels of CER and DAG in Golgi membranes. Does not use free phosphorylcholine or CDP-choline as donor. Regulates receptor-mediated signal transduction via mitogenic DAG and proapoptotic CER, as well as via SM, a structural component of membrane rafts that serve as platforms for signal transduction and protein sorting. Plays a role in secretory transport via regulation of DAG pool at the Golgi apparatus and its downstream effects on PRKD1.
